title: Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass)
body_text: # Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass) You are reviewing the LLM-Wiki 3D/2D graph view. The user has filed many rounds of complaints; the agent has shipped many fixes; the user still says "3d graph viewing experience is still not so good". Your job: tell us **what's still broken** as a third party. ## Setup - Repo: `/Users/neo/Developer/Projects/LLM-Wiki`. You are already there. - The graph view source: `llm_wiki/site/js.py` (`JS_BUNDLE_GRAPH`), `llm_wiki/site/pages.py::render_graph_view`, `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py` (`.graph-page` CSS). - Live compiled output: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/index.html`, `.llm-wiki/site/assets/graph-.js`. - Graph payload: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-core.json` (top 80 nodes by degree, ~88 KB) + `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-rest.json` (rest, ~253 KB). - The user's recent complaints are listed in commit messages from the last ~50 commits — read `git log --oneline -50` for context. Key parameters in the current implementation (don't change them; just diagnose): - Initial camera at `z=1000`. `OrbitControls.maxDistance = 1200`, `minDistance = 35`. - `nodeRelSize = 6`. Node `val = 2 + min(in_degree, 200)**0.92 * 1.4` so a 30-incoming hub renders at val

frontmatter.title: Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass)
frontmatter.summary: # Codex critique: graph view UX (ouroboros pass) You are reviewing the LLM-Wiki 3D/2D graph view. The user has filed many rounds of complaints; the agent has shipped many fixes; the user still says "3d graph viewing experience is still not so good". Your job: tell us **what's still broken** as a third party. ## Setup - Repo: `/Users/neo/Developer/Projects/LLM-Wiki`. You are already there. - The graph view source: `llm_wiki/site/js.py` (`JS_BUNDLE_GRAPH`), `llm_wiki/site/pages.py::render_graph_view`, `llm_wiki/site/tokens.py` (`.graph-page` CSS). - Live compiled output: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/index.html`, `.llm-wiki/site/assets/graph-.js`. - Graph payload: `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-core.json` (top 80 nodes by degree, ~88 KB) + `.llm-wiki/site/graph/payload-rest.json` (rest, ~253 KB). - The user's recent complaints are listed in commit messages from the last ~50 commits — read `git log --oneline -50` for context. Key parameters in the current implementation (don't change them; just diagnose): - Initial camera at `z=1000`. `OrbitControls.maxDistance = 1200`, `minDistance = 35`. - `nodeRelSize = 6`. Node `val = 2 + min(in_degree, 200)**0.92 * 1.4` so a 30-incoming hub renders at val
commands_run: nl -ba llm_wiki/site/pages.py | sed -n '2320,2445p' && nl -ba llm_wiki/site/tokens.py | sed -n '1490,1575p'

python - <<'PY'
import json, math, collections
for name in ['payload-core.json','payload-rest.json','payload.json']:
    p=json.load(open(f'.llm-wiki/site/graph/{name}'))
    nodes=p.get('nodes',[]); links=p.get('links',p.get('edges',[]))
    deg=collections.Counter()
    indeg=collections.Counter()
    groups=collections.Counter(n.get('group') for n in nodes)
    ids={n.get('id') for n in nodes}
    for l in links:
        s=l.get('source'); t=l.get('target')
        if isinstance(s,dict): s=s.get('id')
        if isinstance(t,dict): t=t.get('id')
        deg[s]+=1; deg[t]+=1; indeg[t]+=1
    vals=[]
    for n in nodes:
        vals.append((n.get('val'), n.get('degree'), deg[n.get('id')], indeg[n.get('id')], n.get('name') or n.get('id')))
    vals_sorted=sorted(vals, key=lambda x: (x[0] or 0), reverse=True)[:10]
    print('\n',name, 'nodes',len(nodes),'links',len(links),'groups',groups)
    print('degree max/median', max([v[2] for v in vals] or [0]), sorted([v[2] for v in vals])[len(vals)//2] if vals else 0)
    print('val top', vals_sorted[:8])
    missing=sum(1 for l in links if (l.get('source') if not isinstance(l.get('source'),dict) else l.get('source',{}).get('id')) not in ids or (l.get('target') if not isinstance(l.get('target'),dict) else l.get('target',{}).get('id')) not in ids)
    print('missing endpoint links', missing)
PY

python3 - <<'PY'
import json, math, collections
for name in ['payload-core.json','payload-rest.json','payload.json']:
    p=json.load(open(f'.llm-wiki/site/graph/{name}'))
    nodes=p.get('nodes',[]); links=p.get('links',p.get('edges',[]))
    deg=collections.Counter(); indeg=collections.Counter()
    groups=collections.Counter(n.get('group') for n in nodes)
    ids={n.get('id') for n in nodes}
    for l in links:
        s=l.get('source'); t=l.get('target')
        if isinstance(s,dict): s=s.get('id')
        if isinstance(t,dict): t=t.get('id')
        deg[s]+=1; deg[t]+=1; indeg[t]+=1
    vals=[]
    for n in nodes:
        vals.append((n.get('val'), n.get('degree'), n.get('in_degree'), deg[n.get('id')], indeg[n.get('id')], n.get('name') or n.get('id')))
    vals_sorted=sorted(vals, key=lambda x: (x[0] or 0), reverse=True)[:10]
    print('\n'+name, 'nodes',len(nodes),'links',len(links),'groups',groups)
    print('payload degree max/median', max([v[1] or 0 for v in vals] or [0]), sorted([v[1] or 0 for v in vals])[len(vals)//2] if vals else 0)
    print('local degree max/median', max([v[3] for v in vals] or [0]), sorted([v[3] for v in vals])[len(vals)//2] if vals else 0)
    print('val top')
    for row in vals_sorted[:8]: print(' ', row)
    missing=sum(1 for l in links if (l.get('source') if not isinstance(l.get('source'),dict) else l.get('source',{}).get('id')) not in ids or (l.get('target') if not isinstance(l.get('target'),dict) else l.get('target',{}).get('id')) not in ids)
    print('missing endpoint links', missing)
PY
nl -ba llm_wiki/site/js.py | sed -n '730,815p' && nl -ba llm_wiki/site/js.py | sed -n '1315,1550p'
